powers.name: Bond Famulus
powers.description: Feed the animal your blood on three separate nights to create a ghoul animal familiar. The Famulus is loyal, can be commanded telepathically within line of sight, and does not age. You can see through its senses by concentrating (1 turn). Only one Famulus at a time. If it dies, you can bond a new one after one lunar cycle.
powers.cost: Free (initial bonding costs 1 Rouse per night x3)

powers.name: Sense the Beast
powers.description: By focusing on a target within line of sight, sense whether they carry a supernatural Beast. Detects vampires, werewolves, and other creatures with a predatory aura. A critical win reveals the creature type; a regular success only confirms something inhuman. Does not work through electronic media. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Feral Whispers
powers.description: Communicate telepathically with a single animal you can see. You can issue commands with a Manipulation + Animal Ken roll; the animal follows instructions to the best of its ability but interprets them in animal terms. Complex commands (guard this door, follow that person and return) require more successes. Lasts one scene or until the task is complete. Animals remember you favorably afterward.
powers.cost: Free (1 Rouse to command swarms or multiple animals)

powers.name: Animal Succulence
powers.description: Passive. Animal blood slakes Hunger more effectively — each drink from an animal reduces Hunger by 2 instead of 1. Additionally, you never risk a Bestial Failure when feeding on animals. This does not change the Hunger minimum (Hunger 1 can never be slaked by animals).

powers.name: Quell the Beast
powers.description: Target a vampire or mortal within conversational range. On success, suppress their Beast — vampires cannot frenzy and mortals become docile and passive. The target feels drained and lethargic. Lasts for the scene or until the target takes damage. Contested by the target's Composure + Resolve. On a critical win, the target cannot frenzy for the rest of the night.
powers.pool: Cha + Animal Ken vs Composure + Resolve

powers.name: Unliving Hive
powers.description: You can bond insects and other vermin as Famuli instead of larger animals. A swarm counts as a single Famulus. The swarm can scout, deliver messages, or attack (dealing 1 superficial damage per turn to a target they engulf). You sense through the swarm collectively. The swarm is fragile — fire or area effects destroy it.

powers.name: Subsume the Spirit
powers.description: Project your consciousness into your bonded Famulus, taking full control of its body. Your own body falls into a torpor-like state and is defenseless. You gain the animal's physical capabilities (flight, aquatic movement, etc.) while retaining your mental Attributes. If the animal is killed, you awaken in your body but take 2 Aggravated Willpower damage. Lasts until you choose to return or until sunrise.

powers.name: Animal Dominion
powers.description: Command all animals of a chosen type within approximately 1 mile. Every rat, dog, bird, etc. of that type obeys your will simultaneously. You can direct them to attack (swarm deals 3 Aggravated damage per turn), scout an area, or create diversions. Maintaining control requires concentration — you can take no other actions. Lasts one scene. Extremely conspicuous.

powers.name: Drawing Out the Beast
powers.description: When you would enter frenzy, you can instead project your Beast into another being within line of sight. That target enters frenzy in your place with your Beast driving them — they attack and rage while you remain calm but emotionless (cannot use Willpower, feel no fear or anger). If the target is a vampire, they frenzy normally. If mortal, they go berserk. Lasts until the frenzy would naturally end. If the target is killed or leaves your line of sight, the Beast returns to you and you must immediately roll to resist frenzy at +2 difficulty.
powers.pool: Wits + Animal Ken vs Composure + Resolve

powers.name: Heightened Senses
powers.description: Sharpen one or all senses to supernatural levels. Hear whispered conversations through walls, read fine print at great distance, identify individuals by scent, taste blood resonance with precision. While active, loud noises, bright lights, or strong smells can overwhelm you — take a 2-dice penalty on all actions for 1 turn. Can be activated reflexively. Lasts one scene or until deactivated.
powers.pool: Wits + Awareness (to detect specific hidden things)

powers.name: Sense the Unseen
powers.description: Detect supernatural concealment: Obfuscate, spirit presence, hidden magic. Roll Wits + Auspex against the concealing power's rating. Success reveals the presence and general location of the hidden entity but not their identity. Works passively — the Storyteller may call for a roll when something hidden is nearby. Also detects mortals hiding by mundane means, though with less precision.

powers.name: Premonition
powers.description: Receive involuntary flashes of insight — visions of the near future, warnings of ambushes, or glimpses of important events. The Storyteller may trigger these spontaneously. You can also deliberately meditate on a question (costs 1 Rouse) and receive a symbolic vision. Visions are never literal — they come as metaphors, sensory fragments, or emotional impressions. The Storyteller determines the clarity based on successes rolled.

powers.name: Scry the Soul
powers.description: Read the target's aura — a halo of shifting colors visible only to you. Reveals: emotional state (colors), supernatural nature (pale aura = vampire, bright = mage, etc.), Diablerie stains (black veins), recent feeding (red flush), and whether they are under a Blood Bond (red threads). Each success reveals one piece of information. The target does not know they are being read unless they have Auspex themselves. Range: line of sight.

powers.name: Share the Senses
powers.description: Tap into the senses of a person you have previously touched or whose blood you have tasted. You perceive everything they see, hear, and feel in real time. Your own senses are suppressed while sharing. The target is unaware unless they have Auspex 2+. Range: same city. Duration: one scene or until you voluntarily withdraw. Taking damage in your real body ends the effect.

powers.name: Spirit's Touch
powers.description: Touch an object to read psychic impressions left on it. Receive visions of the last person to handle it — their appearance, emotional state, and what they were doing. Successes determine clarity and how far back the impressions reach: 1 success = last few hours, 3 = last week, 5 = months. Strong emotional events (violence, passion, terror) leave the most vivid impressions. Useful for crime scene investigation.

powers.name: Clairvoyance
powers.description: Project your senses to any location you have previously visited or that contains a person whose blood you have tasted. You see and hear as if physically present but cannot interact. You are invisible and intangible at the remote location. Duration: number of minutes equal to successes rolled. A critical win allows you to also smell and feel at the location. Range: anywhere in the world.

powers.name: Possession
powers.description: Project your mind into a mortal's body, taking complete control. Your own body falls into a torpor-like state. You gain access to the mortal's physical Attributes and skills but use your own mental/social Attributes. The mortal's consciousness is suppressed — they experience the time as a blackout. If the mortal body takes lethal damage, you are ejected and your real body takes 1 Aggravated Willpower damage. Duration: one scene. Cannot possess supernatural beings.

powers.name: Telepathy
powers.description: Read surface thoughts of a target within line of sight. The target is unaware unless they have Auspex. Surface thoughts appear as a stream of impressions — whatever the target is currently thinking about. With 3+ successes, you can dig deeper for specific memories, but the target may feel mental intrusion (Wits + Awareness vs your successes to notice). You can also project thoughts into the target's mind, which they experience as their own internal voice. Cannot be used to command.

powers.name: Corrosive Vitae
powers.description: Transmute your blood into a caustic acid. Spit or smear vitae on a surface to corrode it — dissolves metal locks, weakens doors, burns through rope. Deals 2 Superficial damage (Aggravated to mortals) if used on a living target. The acid remains potent for one turn per success on the Rouse check. Cannot be used on your own body without harming yourself.

powers.name: A Taste for Blood
powers.description: By tasting a drop of blood, learn the following about its source: mortal or supernatural, approximate generation (if Kindred), blood resonance and temperament, whether a Blood Bond exists and roughly how strong. Each success reveals one fact. You can also determine if the blood has been tampered with (poisoned, magically altered). The blood must be relatively fresh (within 24 hours).

powers.name: Extinguish Vitae
powers.description: Target a vampire within line of sight and force their body to consume its own blood. The target immediately makes a Rouse check that they cannot avoid — on a failure, their Hunger increases by 1. You can force additional Rouse checks with additional successes (1 extra check per 2 net successes). If this would push the target to Hunger 5, they must immediately check for Hunger Frenzy. Range: 20 meters.
powers.pool: Int + Blood Sorcery vs Sta + Composure

powers.name: Blood of Potency
powers.description: Temporarily concentrate the power of your vitae, lowering your effective generation by 1 per success (minimum generation 8). This increases your Blood Potency for purposes of power bonuses, blood surge, and mending. Lasts one scene. At the end, make a Rouse check for each level of generation reduced — Hunger may spike dramatically when the effect wears off. Does not affect actual generation for Dominate comparisons.

powers.name: Scorpion's Touch
powers.description: Transmute your blood into a paralytic venom. Coat a weapon, apply to your fangs, or spit it at a target. On contact with a living being, the venom inflicts agonizing pain and reduces the target's Physical dice pools by your Blood Sorcery rating for the scene. Vampires resist with Stamina + Resolve (difficulty = your successes). Mortals are not entitled to a resistance roll. The venom evaporates after one scene if unused.
powers.pool: Str + Blood Sorcery vs Sta + Resolve

powers.name: Theft of Vitae
powers.description: Rip the blood from a target's body at a distance. Crimson streams visibly fly from the victim to you. The target takes 2 Aggravated Health damage as blood is torn from their veins. You reduce your Hunger by 1 per 2 successes on the roll (minimum Hunger 0). Range: line of sight, up to 30 meters. Contested roll — the target can attempt to resist. Mortals who witness this are likely to flee or enter shock. Extremely Masquerade-threatening.
powers.pool: Wits + Blood Sorcery vs Wits + Occult

powers.name: Baal's Caress
powers.description: Coat a melee weapon in your vitae, transforming it into a supernatural instrument of destruction. For the rest of the scene, the weapon deals Aggravated damage to all targets, including vampires. The weapon glows faintly with an inner crimson light. The effect persists until the weapon is sheathed, dropped, or the scene ends. Each attack with the coated weapon costs no additional Rouse — the initial activation is sufficient.

powers.name: Cauldron of Blood
powers.description: Boil the blood inside a target's body. The target must be within line of sight and within 10 meters. The victim's blood literally boils — they take 3 Aggravated damage that cannot be soaked. Vampires lose blood as it evaporates: Hunger increases by 2. If this kills a mortal, their body ruptures. Vampires at Hunger 5 who are hit by this must immediately check for Hunger Frenzy at Difficulty 4. Extremely visible and Masquerade-breaking.
powers.pool: Res + Blood Sorcery vs Sta + Resolve

powers.name: Cat's Grace
powers.description: Passive. You always land on your feet. All balance-related rolls gain a bonus equal to your Celerity rating. You can walk across tightropes, narrow ledges, and slippery surfaces without rolling. Falls from any height deal half damage (round down). Your movements are unnaturally fluid — mortals may notice something 'off' about you on a Wits + Awareness roll (difficulty 4).

powers.name: Rapid Reflexes
powers.description: Passive. You can always act first in a combat turn without rolling initiative — you simply go first among those without this power. If multiple vampires have Rapid Reflexes, they compare Wits + Celerity to determine order among themselves. You can also use your free action to dodge ranged attacks, including firearms, which is normally impossible.

powers.name: Fleetness
powers.description: Add your Celerity rating to your Dexterity for non-combat physical actions (running, dodging, acrobatics) and to your Defense rating in combat. Your movement speed doubles. Lasts one scene. While active, you move with visible supernatural speed — running appears as a blur to mortal observers. This is a Masquerade risk if used openly.

powers.name: Blink
powers.description: Move up to 50 meters in the blink of an eye — literally faster than human perception. You seem to disappear and reappear. Can be used to close distance to an enemy, escape a grapple, or cross a room before anyone can react. Cannot be used to dodge an attack already in progress. Walls and obstacles still block you. Can be used once per turn.

powers.name: Traversal
powers.description: Run along vertical surfaces (walls, building faces) and across liquids (water, pools) as though they were level ground. You must keep moving — stopping causes you to fall or sink normally. You can run up a building face, across a river, or along a ceiling for a number of turns equal to your Celerity rating. Combine with Blink for spectacular movement.

powers.name: Draught of Elegance
powers.description: Feed a mortal or ghoul your blood to grant them Celerity 1 (Cat's Grace or Rapid Reflexes, your choice) for one night. The recipient must drink directly from you (1 Rouse worth). Only one recipient at a time. The recipient does not gain any other vampiric traits. Useful for empowering ghouls or mortal allies before a dangerous situation.

powers.name: Unerring Aim
powers.description: Take one turn to aim. On your next turn, your single ranged attack (firearms, thrown weapons) automatically hits — no roll required. The target cannot dodge (even with Celerity). The shot deals normal damage plus bonus damage equal to your Celerity rating. This power can only be used once per scene. Does not work on targets behind full cover.

powers.name: Lightning Strike
powers.description: Your next melee attack is so fast that the target cannot defend against it — their Defense is reduced to 0. You add your Celerity rating as bonus damage. The attack is invisible to the naked eye; mortals see only the aftermath. Can be used once per turn. Combine with weapons for devastating effect. Cannot be used in the same turn as Blink.

powers.name: Split Second
powers.description: Take two full actions in a single combat turn. This includes two attacks, an attack and a power activation, or any other combination. Each action is resolved separately with its own dice pool. You may even split movement between actions. However, both actions suffer a 2-dice penalty. This power can be used every turn but each use costs a Rouse check. If combined with Fleetness, the dice penalty is reduced to 1.

powers.name: Cloud Memory
powers.description: Erase the last few minutes (up to about 5 minutes) from a mortal's memory with the phrase 'Forget.' The target loses all recollection of the erased period and fills the gap with plausible mundane memories. Works automatically on mortals. Vampires of equal or higher generation can resist with Wits + Resolve vs your pool. Requires eye contact. Does not work through electronic media.

powers.name: Compel
powers.description: Issue a single short command (maximum 2-3 words) that the target must immediately obey: 'Stop,' 'Run,' 'Drop it,' 'Sleep,' 'Speak.' The command must be something the target could physically accomplish. Self-destructive commands ('Die') fail automatically. The compulsion lasts one turn. Requires eye contact and the target must hear and understand you. Vampires of higher generation are immune.

powers.name: Mesmerize
powers.description: Implant a complex suggestion or post-hypnotic command in the target's mind. The command can include triggers ('When you see the Prince, hand them this envelope') and can be multi-step. The target carries out the command believing it to be their own idea. The suggestion lasts until fulfilled or until the next sunrise, whichever comes first. More successes = more complex commands accepted. Commands that endanger the target grant them a Willpower roll to resist. Requires unbroken eye contact during implantation (about 1 minute).

powers.name: Dementation
powers.description: Inflict temporary madness on a target. Choose a mental derangement (paranoia, hallucinations, catatonia, uncontrollable rage, etc.) — the target suffers from it for the remainder of the scene. Mortals may develop lasting trauma. Vampires shake it off at dawn. The specific manifestation is influenced by the target's Nature and existing psychological vulnerabilities. Requires eye contact. Contested — target resists with Composure + Int.

powers.name: The Forgetful Mind
powers.description: Rewrite or completely remove memories from a target's mind. You can alter memories of events going back months or years. The process takes about 5 minutes of unbroken eye contact per memory being altered. More successes = more seamless the alteration. Poorly crafted memories (1-2 successes) may feel 'wrong' to the target and could unravel under therapy or Auspex. A critical win creates memories indistinguishable from real ones. Multiple sessions can rewrite entire life histories. This is a powerful Masquerade tool.

powers.name: Submerged Directive
powers.description: Implant a command that lies dormant until triggered by a specific condition you define: 'When you hear church bells, walk to Elysium.' The trigger can be a time, event, phrase, or sensory experience. The command can be complex (several sentences). The target is completely unaware of the implanted directive until it triggers. Once triggered, it functions as Mesmerize. The directive lasts indefinitely until triggered, but fades after one lunar cycle if not activated. Only one Submerged Directive per target at a time.

powers.name: Rationalize
powers.description: Passive enhancement to all your Dominate powers. Targets of your Dominate no longer feel that they were commanded — they genuinely believe the implanted thoughts and actions were their own ideas. They will rationalize and defend the behavior if questioned. This makes Dominate nearly undetectable by mundane means. Even Auspex requires 3+ successes to identify the influence. This applies to Compel, Mesmerize, Submerged Directive, and Forgetful Mind.

powers.name: Mass Manipulation
powers.description: Use Compel or Mesmerize on a crowd of mortals simultaneously (up to approximately 20 people). You must be able to address the crowd — a speech, announcement, or broadcast. All targets must hear you. Roll once; the result applies to all targets. Individual targets with Resolve 4+ may resist separately. Cannot be used on crowds containing vampires (use normal Dominate for them individually). Duration and effects as per the base power used.

powers.name: Terminal Decree
powers.description: Implant a command so deeply that violating it causes the target physical harm or death. The target who breaks the command suffers: mortals take a heart attack (potentially lethal), vampires take 3 Aggravated Willpower damage and must check for frenzy. Alternatively, you can implant a command that the target literally cannot disobey — their body refuses to act against it. Requires 10 minutes of unbroken eye contact and an extended contested roll. The decree lasts until broken or removed by a Dominate user of higher generation.

powers.name: Resilience
powers.description: Passive. Add your Fortitude rating as extra Health levels specifically for tracking Superficial damage. These bonus levels are lost first when taking damage. They regenerate at the start of each night. This effectively increases your ability to absorb punishment before being impaired.

powers.name: Unswayable Mind
powers.description: Passive. Add your Fortitude rating as bonus dice to resist all supernatural mental influence: Dominate, Presence, Dementation, and similar powers. This also applies to mundane persuasion enhanced by blood (Blood Bond influence). Does not protect against Auspex reading, only against powers that compel or alter your mental state.

powers.name: Toughness
powers.description: Convert Aggravated damage from non-supernatural physical sources (falls, car crashes, gunfire, blades) to Superficial damage. Each point of Aggravated becomes 2 Superficial instead. Does not protect against fire, sunlight, or supernatural attacks. Lasts one scene. While active, you can shrug off impacts that would pulverize a mortal.

powers.name: Enduring Beasts
powers.description: Grant your ghouls and bonded animals (Famuli) Fortitude 1 (Resilience) permanently as long as they regularly receive your blood. They gain the bonus Health levels and general hardiness. If the blood bond lapses, the protection fades over one week. Useful for protecting mortal retainers and animal companions in dangerous situations.

powers.name: Defy Bane
powers.description: Resist fire and sunlight — the two things that deal Aggravated damage to all vampires. When exposed, roll Wits + Fortitude (difficulty 3 for a torch, 4 for a bonfire, 5 for direct sunlight). Success converts the Aggravated damage to Superficial for that turn. Each turn of continued exposure requires a new roll. Also reduces Rotschreck difficulties by your Fortitude rating. Does not make you immune, but can mean the difference between Final Death and survival.

powers.name: Fortify the Inner Facade
powers.description: Mask your aura from Auspex. When someone attempts Scry the Soul on you, they read a false aura of your choosing — you can appear mortal, emotionless, or display any emotion you want. They receive no indication the aura is false unless they score a critical win, in which case they sense 'something is off.' Also protects against casual emotional reading. Lasts one night.

powers.name: Draught of Endurance
powers.description: Feed a mortal or ghoul your blood to grant them Fortitude 1 (Resilience or Unswayable Mind, your choice) for one night. The recipient must drink directly from you. Only one recipient at a time. Useful for preparing mortal allies to survive dangerous confrontations or resist mental manipulation.

powers.name: Flesh of Marble
powers.description: Your skin becomes supernaturally hard — visibly stone-like in texture. All physical damage (melee, firearms, falls) is halved before being applied. Aggravated physical damage is converted to Superficial. You become nearly immune to conventional weapons. Lasts one scene. Drawback: your Dexterity-based dice pools suffer a 2-dice penalty due to reduced flexibility, and your appearance is obviously inhuman. Fire and sunlight damage is unaffected.

powers.name: Prowess from Pain
powers.description: Passive. Each Health level of damage you have marked (both Superficial and Aggravated) adds 1 bonus die to your physical dice pools instead of penalizing you. The more hurt you are, the stronger you become. At the Impaired level, instead of halving your dice, you add your total damage as bonus dice. This does not prevent Final Death — it just means you fight hardest when closest to destruction.

powers.name: Cloak of Shadows
powers.description: Become invisible while stationary in an area of shadow or low light. You do not literally vanish — minds simply fail to register your presence. You can be detected by Auspex (Sense the Unseen) or by bumping into someone. Making noise, speaking, attacking, or moving more than a slow shuffle breaks the effect. Does not work in bright light or if someone is already looking directly at you when you activate it.

powers.name: Silence of Death
powers.description: Suppress all sounds you produce — footsteps, breathing, rustling clothing, even speech becomes whisper-quiet. You can still choose to speak audibly. Affects only sounds generated by your body and worn equipment. Doesn't silence environmental noise you cause (knocking over a vase, opening a creaky door). Lasts one scene. Combined with Cloak of Shadows, makes you nearly undetectable.

powers.name: Unseen Passage
powers.description: Move while remaining invisible. Walk, run, climb — as long as you don't interact with anyone directly, you remain unseen. Opening a door, picking up an object, or bumping into someone may briefly reveal you (Wits + Obfuscate to maintain). You leave no footprints and disturb no curtains. Attacking or speaking breaks the effect. Cameras and electronic surveillance are not fooled unless you also have Ghost in the Machine.

powers.name: Ghost in the Machine
powers.description: Extend your Obfuscate to electronic devices — cameras, microphones, motion sensors fail to detect you. You do not appear on recordings, thermal cameras read no heat signature, and motion detectors don't trigger. This is passive while any other Obfuscate power is active. Does not affect live observers watching monitors in real time — only the electronic recording/detection itself.

powers.name: Mask of a Thousand Faces
powers.description: Appear as a completely different person. You choose the appearance — it can be anyone of roughly your build (1-foot height difference is the maximum). The disguise is mental, not physical — mirrors and cameras show your real face (unless Ghost in the Machine is active). The persona is convincing: face, voice, mannerisms, and even perceived clothing change. People who know the impersonated individual well may get a Wits + Awareness roll to sense something wrong. Creating a specific person's appearance (rather than a generic stranger) requires you to have studied them.

powers.name: Vanish
powers.description: Disappear from plain sight while people are watching you. Unlike Cloak of Shadows, this works in full light and while you are the center of attention. Observers blink and you are simply gone — they can't recall you leaving. This is a reflexive action that can be triggered at any time, even mid-conversation. Once invisible, you function as per Unseen Passage. Extremely useful for escape and dramatic exits. Auspex users contest with Wits + Auspex.
powers.pool: Wits + Obfuscate (vs Wits + Auspex if resisted)

powers.name: Conceal
powers.description: Make an inanimate object invisible — a car, a coffin, a door, a weapon. The object can be up to the size of a small vehicle. Observers' eyes slide past it as though it doesn't exist. The effect lasts one scene or until you end it. You can conceal an object and walk away — it remains hidden. Touching the concealed object doesn't reveal it, but actively searching for it with Auspex can detect it.

powers.name: Cloak the Gathering
powers.description: Extend any of your Obfuscate powers to a group of up to (Wits + Obfuscate) people. Everyone in the group becomes invisible or disguised as you choose. All members must stay within about 10 meters of you — anyone who strays beyond this radius becomes visible. If any member of the group attacks or causes a commotion, only that individual is revealed. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Impostor's Guise
powers.description: A perfected version of Mask of a Thousand Faces. You take on the exact appearance of a specific individual you have studied closely (at least 1 hour of observation or a drink of their blood). The disguise includes voice, mannerisms, gait, and even subtle habits. Unlike Mask, this disguise is physical — it fools mirrors, cameras, and even Auspex below level 4. Even intimate associates must roll Wits + Awareness at Difficulty 5 to detect the impersonation. Duration: one night.

powers.name: Shadow Cloak
powers.description: Wrap yourself in a layer of living shadow. Grants +2 dice to Stealth rolls in darkness. In dim light, you appear as a deeper shadow — unsettling but not obviously supernatural. The shadow cloak also dampens sounds slightly. If examined closely in bright light, the shadows are visibly unnatural. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Oblivion's Sight
powers.description: Peer through the Shroud into the Shadowlands. You see ghostly echoes of the dead overlaid on reality — spirits, haunts, and remnants of death. You can identify recently deceased individuals, sense how someone died at a location, and see ghosts that are present but invisible. While active, the living world appears dimmer and the dead brighter. You can communicate with visible ghosts but cannot physically interact with them. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Ashes to Ashes
powers.description: Touch a corpse (mortal only) to reduce it to fine ash in seconds. The ash is inert and indistinguishable from wood ash. All forensic evidence is destroyed — dental records, DNA, fingerprints, everything. Does not work on undead, staked vampires, or living beings. The corpse must be relatively fresh (within one week). Extremely useful for Masquerade cleanup.

powers.name: Arms of Ahriman
powers.description: Summon tendrils of solid shadow from any nearby dark surface. The tendrils can grapple, restrain, or attack targets within 10 meters. Each tendril has Strength equal to your Oblivion rating. You can summon a number of tendrils equal to your successes. Grappled targets must beat the tendril's Strength with their own Strength + Athletics to break free. Tendrils deal 1 Superficial damage per turn to grappled targets. Bright light (spotlight, sunlight) destroys the tendrils instantly. Lasts one scene or until destroyed.

powers.name: Where the Shroud Thins
powers.description: Passively sense the presence of ghosts, haunted locations, and areas where the barrier between the living and dead worlds is weak. You feel a cold chill and an emotional echo of the type of death associated with the location. Can also detect Oblivion use by other vampires (Hecata, Lasombra). Range: approximately 50 meters. Does not reveal precise locations — only a general awareness of 'something dead is near.'

powers.name: Touch of Oblivion
powers.description: Channel entropy through your touch, dealing 2 Aggravated damage to a target. The touch causes visible decay — flesh withers, metal rusts, wood rots. Against vampires, the damage manifests as corpse-like decay on the struck area. Against inanimate objects, this can destroy locks, weaken structural supports, or rot through ropes. Requires a successful melee attack (Str + Brawl) to land the touch.

powers.name: Aura of Decay
powers.description: Emanate an aura of entropy in a 5-meter radius. All organic matter ages and decays — flowers wilt, food rots, wood warps, leather cracks. Living beings (mortals and animals) in the area take 1 Superficial damage per turn and feel intense nausea and despair. Vampires are unaffected physically but feel their Beast stir (+1 difficulty to frenzy checks). Lasts a number of turns equal to successes rolled.

powers.name: Shadow Cast
powers.description: Animate your own shadow to act independently. The shadow can separate from your body and move up to 20 meters away. It can manipulate light objects (up to 5 lbs), scout around corners, and deliver whispered messages. The shadow has your Wits and Dexterity for relevant rolls but cannot deal damage. If the shadow is 'destroyed' by bright light, you take 1 Superficial Willpower damage. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Stygian Shroud
powers.description: Fill a room-sized area (up to 30 meters radius) with impenetrable supernatural darkness. No natural or artificial light functions within — flashlights, fire, and even headlights are snuffed out. Only Oblivion's Sight or Auspex (Heightened Senses) can see through it. Everyone inside without such powers is effectively blind (all visual-based pools reduced to 0). The darkness is cold and oppressive — mortals must roll Courage or flee in panic. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Necromantic Binding
powers.description: Bind a ghost to your will. The ghost must be present (use Oblivion's Sight to see it). Contested by the ghost's Resolve + Awareness. On success, the ghost must obey your commands for one lunar cycle. You can command it to haunt a location, spy on people, attack targets (ghosts can manifest to terrify or poltergeist objects), or answer questions about the afterlife. Treat the ghost as having dice pools equal to its pre-death stats. The ghost is resentful and will exploit loopholes in your commands.
powers.pool: Cha + Oblivion vs ghost's Resolve + Awareness

powers.name: Withering Spirit
powers.description: Attack the very essence of a target — their soul or vampiric Beast. The target takes 3 Aggravated Willpower damage (not Health). If this empties their Willpower track, mortals fall into a permanent catatonic state; vampires enter involuntary torpor for a duration determined by their Humanity. The attack is invisible — observers see the target simply collapse. Range: touch. Devastating against enemies you can get close to.

powers.name: Shadowstep
powers.description: Step into one shadow and emerge from another within line of sight (up to 100 meters). The transition is instant — you dissolve into darkness and reconstitute. You can carry anything you could normally carry. Can be used in combat as movement. The destination shadow must be large enough to contain your body (at least as large as a doorway's shadow). Cannot be used in areas without shadows or under direct bright light. Can be used once per turn.

powers.name: Lethal Body
powers.description: Passive. Your unarmed attacks (punches, kicks, bites) deal Aggravated damage to mortals instead of Superficial. Against vampires, your unarmed attacks still deal Superficial damage but cannot be halved (they are treated as if from a weapon). Your grip strength is similarly enhanced — you can crush metal cans, bend bars, and leave handprint indentations in wood.

powers.name: Soaring Leap
powers.description: Passive. You can jump extraordinary distances — vertically up to 3 meters per Potence dot, horizontally up to 5 meters per Potence dot, from a standing start. Running jumps double these distances. You always land safely regardless of height. This allows you to reach rooftops, clear fences, and leap between buildings. Witnesses will obviously notice something superhuman.

powers.name: Prowess
powers.description: Add your Potence rating as bonus dice to all Strength-based rolls: melee damage, feats of strength (lifting, breaking, throwing), and grappling. Also add Potence as bonus Superficial damage to all melee attacks. With this active, you can flip cars, punch through concrete walls, and throw grown adults across rooms. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Brutal Feed
powers.description: Passive. When you feed on a mortal, you can drain them completely in a single turn (normally takes several). The target takes 3 Aggravated damage from the savage feeding and is left in critical condition. You reduce your Hunger by 3 instead of the normal amount. The feeding is messy and violent — the victim is left with obvious wounds. Useful in emergencies but a severe Masquerade risk.

powers.name: Spark of Rage
powers.description: Touch a target (requires a successful Brawl attack) and flood their Beast with rage. Mortals fly into an uncontrollable violent rage for the rest of the scene, attacking the nearest person. Vampires must immediately make a Frenzy check at Difficulty 4. Even on success, the vampire suffers a 2-dice penalty to all non-violent actions for the rest of the scene due to simmering rage. Cannot be used on the same target more than once per night.

powers.name: Uncanny Grip
powers.description: Passive. Cling to any surface — walls, ceilings, glass — with supernatural grip. You can walk vertically, hang upside down, or maintain position on a sheer cliff face with no roll required. You can support an additional person's weight while climbing. The grip cannot be broken by non-supernatural means. Useful for ambushes, infiltration, and dramatic entrances.

powers.name: Draught of Might
powers.description: Feed a mortal or ghoul your blood to grant them Potence 1 (Lethal Body or Soaring Leap, your choice) for one night. The recipient must drink directly from you. Only one recipient at a time. The recipient's eyes may take on a slightly reddish tint while the power is active. Useful for creating a temporary bodyguard or empowering an ally.

powers.name: Earthshock
powers.description: Stamp your foot or strike the ground with devastating force. Everyone within 10 meters (except you) must roll Dex + Athletics (Difficulty = your successes) or be knocked prone. Prone targets lose their next action getting up. The impact cracks concrete, shatters windows, and sets off car alarms. Structures may be damaged — wooden floors collapse, plaster walls crack. Deals 2 Superficial damage to everyone in the area. Cannot be used indoors above the ground floor without risking structural collapse.

powers.name: Fist of Caine
powers.description: Channel all your Potence into a single devastating melee strike. The attack deals Aggravated damage equal to your Strength + Potence (total, not additional). This can stake a vampire in one blow, crush a skull, or punch through an engine block. The attack cannot be split — it is one strike. If it misses, the power is not expended. Can be used once per scene. The sheer force of the blow produces a visible shockwave.

powers.name: Awe
powers.description: Radiate an aura of magnetic charisma. Everyone within earshot finds you fascinating, attractive, and worth listening to. They will turn to face you, stop conversations, and give you their full attention. In social situations, add your Presence rating as bonus dice to all Charisma and Manipulation rolls. Does not compel obedience — people listen eagerly but can still disagree. Lasts one scene. Vampires resist by adding Composure to their opposing pools.

powers.name: Daunt
powers.description: Project an aura of menace and dominance. Everyone nearby feels uncomfortable, intimidated, and reluctant to challenge you. Add your Presence rating as bonus dice to Intimidation rolls. Mortals with Resolve 2 or lower will not voluntarily approach you or make eye contact. In combat, targets must spend 1 Willpower to attack you for the first time each scene. Does not affect vampires of lower generation. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Lingering Kiss
powers.description: Passive. Your Kiss (vampire bite) produces intense, addictive euphoria far beyond the norm. Mortals you feed from become emotionally obsessed with you and the experience. They gain the Infatuated condition toward you for (your Presence rating) weeks after a single feeding. Repeated feedings deepen the addiction. The mortal will seek you out, defend you, and may neglect their own life. This is not a Blood Bond — it is psychological addiction.

powers.name: Dread Gaze
powers.description: Lock eyes with a target and project overwhelming supernatural terror. The target must roll Composure + Resolve (Difficulty = your successes) or be affected: failure by 1-2 = frozen in place for 1 turn; failure by 3+ = flee in panic for the rest of the scene; critical failure = collapse in gibbering terror. Vampires who fail must check for Rotschreck. Only affects one target at a time. The target remembers the fear vividly. Range: line of sight.

powers.name: Entrancement
powers.description: Target a single person within conversational range. On success, they become utterly devoted to you for (successes) nights. They will do nearly anything you ask — fetch, carry, spy, lie, betray friends — as long as it doesn't directly threaten their life. They view you as the most important person in their world. Self-destructive or suicidal commands break the effect. This is not mind control — the target is willing and believes in their devotion. Contested by the target's Composure + Wits.

powers.name: Irresistible Voice
powers.description: Passive enhancement. Your Presence powers now work through any medium that carries your voice — phone calls, radio, podcasts, live video. The target must hear your actual voice (recordings work but are less effective: -2 dice). This dramatically extends the range and applicability of Awe, Daunt, Dread Gaze, and Entrancement. Does not work through text.

powers.name: Summon
powers.description: Call a person you have previously met to come to your current location. The target feels an overwhelming compulsion to travel to you by the fastest means available. They may not know why — they just feel a desperate need to be at your location. The target travels for up to one night; if they haven't arrived by dawn, the compulsion fades. The target knows your current location intuitively. Range: same city (approximately). Vampires of lower generation can resist with Composure + Int.

powers.name: Majesty
powers.description: Radiate an aura of absolute authority and power. No one in your presence can take hostile action against you — they physically cannot raise a hand, draw a weapon, or speak against you. Mortals drop to their knees. Vampires freeze. To break through Majesty and attack, a vampire must spend a Willpower point and succeed on a Resolve + Composure roll (Difficulty = your Presence + 2). Even on success, they suffer a 3-dice penalty to attack you. Lasts one scene or until you attack someone (which breaks the aura).

powers.name: Star Magnetism
powers.description: Enhance Awe to affect an entire crowd — hundreds of people at once. Everyone in earshot becomes captivated by you. You are the most interesting, beautiful, important person in the room. Crowds part for you, strangers offer gifts and favors, and no one can look away. Useful for public speaking, performances, and creating fanatical followings. The effect fades after the scene, but attendees remember you fondly and will speak of you for weeks. Vampires in the crowd resist individually.

powers.name: Eyes of the Beast
powers.description: Your eyes glow red, granting perfect vision in total darkness — no light source required. You can see in pitch-black conditions as clearly as daylight. The red glow is visible to others and cannot be suppressed. Also grants limited infrared vision — living beings glow faintly with body heat. Does not penetrate supernatural darkness (Stygian Shroud). Can be activated and deactivated reflexively.

powers.name: Weight of the Feather
powers.description: Reduce your effective weight to almost nothing. You leave no footprints, don't trigger pressure plates, and can walk on surfaces that wouldn't support normal weight (thin ice, a pile of leaves, fresh snow). You can also reduce fall damage to 0 for falls up to 30 meters. You still have normal mass for combat purposes — you can be pushed, grappled, and hit normally. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Feral Weapons
powers.description: Grow razor-sharp claws from your fingertips. The claws deal Aggravated damage (Str + 2) to all targets, including vampires. They can rend metal, shred body armor, and tear through car doors. The transformation takes one turn and is painful but brief. Claws can be retracted at will. While extended, fine manipulation (typing, lock-picking) suffers a 3-dice penalty. The claws are obviously inhuman — a clear Masquerade breach if witnessed.

powers.name: Earth Meld
powers.description: Sink into bare earth, soil, or natural stone (not concrete, tile, or pavement). You are completely protected from sunlight, fire, and physical attacks while submerged. You remain aware of activity directly above you (vibrations, sounds). You can emerge at any time as a free action. If the earth above you is disturbed (excavation), you are forced out and must act immediately. Cannot be used in buildings. Staking yourself before melding provides a secure daytime resting place.

powers.name: Shapechange
powers.description: Transform into an animal — typically a wolf, large dog, owl, or bat (choose one form when you first learn this power; you can learn additional forms later). You gain the animal's physical capabilities (flight for a bat, keen senses for a wolf) while retaining your mental Attributes and Disciplines (except those requiring speech or hands). Your clothing and small personal items merge into the form. You can speak telepathically to people who have tasted your blood. Transformation takes 1 turn. Lasts until you choose to revert.

powers.name: Fleshcrafting
powers.description: Reshape flesh and bone with your hands — your own or another's. Can alter facial features (perfect disguise), repair cosmetic damage, add or remove bulk, and sculpt bodies into new configurations. Changes are permanent unless reversed. Working on yourself takes 1 turn for minor changes, 10 minutes for major alterations. Working on others requires them to be restrained or willing. Can be used to inflict horrific disfigurement: 2 Aggravated damage and permanent appearance changes. Historically a Tzimisce art.
powers.pool: Dex + Protean (Dex + Medicine for complex work)

powers.name: Metamorphosis
powers.description: Take on monstrous hybrid forms — a wolf-man, a giant bat creature, or a chitinous insectoid horror. In war-form, gain: +3 to Strength, +2 to Stamina, claws dealing Aggravated damage (Str + 3), and armor equivalent to 2 points of damage reduction. Your appearance is terrifying — mortals must check for panic, and even vampires feel their Beast stir. The transformation takes 1 turn. You cannot speak normally but can growl and snarl. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Horrid Form
powers.description: Transform into a monstrous Zulo shape — a 2.5-meter-tall nightmare of stretched skin, elongated limbs, and exposed muscle. Gain: +4 Strength, +2 Stamina, +1 Dexterity, claws (Str + 3, Aggravated), and a terror aura (mortals flee, vampires must check for Rotschreck at Difficulty 3). You cannot use Social Attributes for anything except Intimidation. The form is unmistakably inhuman. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Mist Form
powers.description: Dissolve your entire body into an intangible cloud of cold mist. In this form, you are immune to all physical damage, sunlight, and fire. You can seep through cracks, keyholes, and ventilation systems. You cannot interact with the physical world, use Disciplines (except Auspex), or speak. Strong winds can disperse you (treat as 2 Aggravated damage). Holy objects block your passage. Reforming takes 1 turn. You can maintain Mist Form indefinitely but each scene requires a Rouse check.

powers.name: The Unfettered Heart
powers.description: Passive. You can shift the location of your heart within your body at will, making staking almost impossible. Any staking attempt suffers a +3 Difficulty penalty. Even if someone knows about this power, they cannot predict where your heart currently is — it moves freely within your torso. Additionally, if you are staked, you can spend 1 Willpower to force the stake out over 3 turns of concentration.

powers.name: Far Reach
powers.description: Telekinetically move objects at a distance. You can manipulate objects up to 10 meters away as if using one hand with Strength 1. This increases to Strength 2 at Alchemy 3 and Strength 3 at Alchemy 5. You can open doors, pull triggers, throw switches, and hurl small objects. Cannot be used to grapple or deal direct damage. Requires concentration — performing other actions while maintaining Far Reach imposes a 2-dice penalty to both. Lasts one scene.

powers.name: Haze
powers.description: Create a cloud of thick, cold fog centered on yourself. The fog fills a 10-meter radius and reduces visibility to arm's length. All ranged attacks through the fog suffer a 3-dice penalty. The fog is obviously unnatural if produced indoors or on a clear night. You can see normally within your own fog. The fog moves with you unless you choose to leave it stationary. Lasts one scene or until dispersed by strong wind.

powers.name: Envelop
powers.description: Direct your Haze to surround a specific target, filling their lungs and eyes. Mortals choke and are blinded: -3 dice to all actions. Vampires are blinded but not suffocated. The target must roll Str + Athletics (Difficulty = your successes) each turn to move out of the cloud. Range: the target must be within your existing Haze. Moving the Envelop to a new target requires a new activation.

powers.name: Profane Hieros Gamos
powers.description: Perform an alchemical ritual to brew a potion from your blood and mundane ingredients. The potion can replicate the effect of a single Level 1 Discipline power from any discipline. The brewing takes 1 hour and produces one dose that remains potent for one night. Anyone who drinks the potion gains the power for one scene. The specific power replicated must be chosen during brewing and requires appropriate ingredients (determined by the Storyteller).

powers.name: Defractionate
powers.description: Separate your vitae into its mystical components through an alchemical process. This allows you to create specialized tinctures: healing draughts (restore 2 Superficial Health damage), courage elixirs (+2 to frenzy resistance), alertness serums (+2 to Perception for one scene), or vitae purifiers (cleanse blood of toxins or Blood Bond influence). Each tincture takes 30 minutes to prepare and lasts one night. You can prepare a number of tinctures per night equal to your Alchemy rating.

powers.name: Airborne Momentum
powers.description: Grant yourself the ability to fly or levitate. Movement speed equals your walking speed. You can hover stationary, ascend vertically, and maneuver in three dimensions. Maximum altitude: approximately 100 meters. Flying is obviously supernatural and a major Masquerade concern. In combat, airborne targets are harder to hit with melee (+2 Difficulty). Lasts one scene. If the power ends while airborne, you fall normally.

powers.name: Counterfeit Discipline (Lesser)
powers.description: Brew a potion that grants a Level 1-2 Discipline power from any discipline to the drinker. The potion takes 2 hours to brew, requires specialized ingredients related to the power being replicated (fangs for Potence, a predator's eyes for Auspex, etc.), and remains potent for one week. The drinker gains the chosen power for one scene per dose. You can have multiple potions prepared simultaneously. Each potion requires its own Rouse check to create.

powers.name: Counterfeit Discipline (Greater)
powers.description: As Counterfeit Discipline (Lesser), but can replicate Level 3-4 Discipline powers. The brewing process takes 4 hours and requires rare, expensive, or morally questionable ingredients. The potion lasts for 3 nights before losing potency. Higher-level powers are more difficult to counterfeit: the brewing roll is made at +2 Difficulty. Failing the roll wastes the ingredients and the Rouse check. Can replicate any Discipline power except Level 5 powers and Amalgam powers.
